name: Parcha Bozbash (Lamb Soup with Chestnuts Quin
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Boneless lean lamb, cut into 1-inch cubes
1/2 cup Dried pitted prunes OR 1 cup Fresh sour prunes
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the lamb with salt and pepper.
2. In a heavy pot, melt the 3 tbsp butter over moderate heat.
3. Add the lamb and onion; sauté until browned, stirring frequently.
4. Add the beef broth; season to taste with salt.
5. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes.
6. Add the potato, quince, prunes, and chestnuts. Cover and simmer for 20 minutes.
7. Add the chick-peas and simmer, covered, about 15 minutes or until done.
8. Stir in the clarified butter and allow to melt before serving soup.
Note: A pinch of powdered saffron or 1/4 tsp ginger dissolved in 2 tbsp warm water may be added with the chick-peas. The soup can be served sprinkled with crushed dried mint, accompanied by chopped onion and sumakh on the side. In the Caucasus, ground kyurdyuk is normally used instead of clarified butter, and dried chick-peas are soaked several hours, drained, and added at the beginning with the broth. Personal note: I've found sumakh (labelled as sumac, a spice mixture) at Middle Eastern groceries. The author describes this soup as an "outstanding" Azerbaidzhani soup.
